success_surface: Successful extraction and transformation of date information from unstructured text strings, combined with targeted cell-level styling and value updates.
effective_behaviors: Use regex pattern matching to extract structured data from inconsistent text formats
Create lookup dictionaries for categorical transformations (e.g., month abbreviations to numbers)
Handle boundary conditions and edge cases (e.g., year rollover when incrementing months)
Apply formatting styles concurrently with data writing operations for efficiency
Target specific row/column ranges based on instruction-specified answer positions
Validate cell values before processing to handle empty or missing data gracefully
Use openpyxl for direct cell-level manipulation requiring both value and style changes
Preserve source file integrity by saving modified workbook to separate output path
Provide clear execution feedback with status messages about completed operations
evidence: Used regex pattern r'(?:[-]?\s*)?([A-Za-z]{3})\s*(\d{2})$' to extract month abbreviation and 2-digit year from variable-length text strings
Created MONTH_MAP dictionary mapping 3-letter month abbreviations to numeric values for conversion logic
Implemented conditional logic for December→January transition with year increment: 'if new_month_num > 12: new_month_num = 1; new_year = (new_year + 1) % 100'
Applied PatternFill directly within iteration loop alongside value assignment: 'cell_d.fill = yellow_fill' concurrent with 'cell_d.value = 21'
Iterated over range(2, 7) corresponding to specified answer position E2:E6 rather than entire worksheet
Checked 'if cell_a:' before processing to avoid errors on empty or NaN cells
Selected openpyxl over pandas for tasks requiring simultaneous value modification and style application
Saved to '/mnt/data/outputs/' path instead of overwriting original source file

memory_items.title: Text Extraction with Regular Expressions
memory_items.lesson: When dealing with unstructured or inconsistent text formats containing embedded structured data, use regex patterns rather than Excel functions or simple string splitting.
memory_items.generalization: Identify consistent patterns in the text (e.g., position, delimiters, character sequences) and construct appropriate regex capture groups to extract target information reliably.

memory_items.title: Mapping Dictionary Strategy for Transformations
memory_items.lesson: Create bidirectional lookup structures (dicts/lists) for conversions between human-readable categories and numeric/logical representations.
memory_items.generalization: Maintain parallel mappings (abbreviation→number, number→full_name) and validate lookups against keys before accessing to prevent runtime errors.

memory_items.title: Boundary Condition Handling
memory_items.lesson: Always check for overflow/underflow scenarios when incrementing cyclic values (months, days, hours).
memory_items.generalization: Implement wrapping logic that resets to minimum value and adjusts dependent fields (e.g., month 12→1 should also increment year).

memory_items.title: Concurrent Style and Data Operations
memory_items.lesson: When using openpyxl, apply cell styles (fill, border, font) immediately after setting cell values in the same iteration.
memory_items.generalization: Batch related modifications per cell rather than separating value updates and style applications across multiple passes.

memory_items.title: Range-Constrained Cell Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Respect instruction-specified answer positions and limit operations to those exact cell ranges.
memory_items.generalization: Parse cell range notations (e.g., E2:E6) to determine start/end rows and columns, then iterate only within those bounds.

memory_items.title: Defensive Empty Value Checking
memory_items.lesson: Verify cell values exist and are non-empty before performing parsing or transformation logic.
memory_items.generalization: Wrap processing code in conditional checks for None, NaN, or empty strings to prevent exceptions on incomplete data.

edits.content:
## 6. Text Parsing & Pattern Matching
- **Unstructured Text**: When extracting structured data from inconsistent text strings, use regex pattern matching instead of relying on fixed delimiters or Excel's built-in functions.
- **Pattern Design**: Identify recurring character sequences and build capture groups for each component (e.g., month abbreviation, year digits).
- **Lookup Tables**: Create bidirectional mapping dictionaries for category conversions; always validate keys before lookup to prevent key errors.

## 7. Edge Case Management
- **Boundary Conditions**: Implement wrap-around logic for cyclic values (months, quarters); adjust dependent fields when cycling occurs.
- **Empty Data**: Check cell values for None/NaN before processing; skip invalid entries gracefully with logging.
- **Mixed Operations**: Apply value changes and cell styles in the same iteration pass for performance and consistency.

## 8. Scope Control
- **Answer Position Awareness**: Parse cell range specifications and limit all modifications to those exact boundaries.
- **Selective Processing**: Do not process the entire sheet unless instructed; respect instruction-type distinctions (Cell-Level vs Sheet-Level).
- **Output Destination**: Always save results to the specified output path; never overwrite the original file without explicit permission.
